definitions.text: At a time in advance of the usual or expected event.
examples: at eleven, we went for an early lunch; she began reading at an early age; his mother suffered an early death

definitions.text: Arriving a time before expected; sooner than on time.
examples: You're early today! I don't usually see you before nine o'clock.
The early guests sipped their punch and avoided each other's eyes.

definitions.text: After but close to the start of a period of time.
examples: The play "Two Gentlemen of Verona" is one of Shakespeare's early works.
Early results showed their winning 245 out of 300 seats in parliament. The main opponent locked up only 31 seats.

definitions.text: A shift (scheduled work period) that takes place early in the day.
At a time before expected; sooner than usual.
examples: We finished the project an hour sooner than scheduled, so we left early.
I had children too early (in life), so my first baby arrived early.
